title: Suppression-safe disparities breakdowns (income/insurance/race-eth/urban-rural)
context: Disparities in cancer screening coverage by income, insurance status, race/ethnicity, and urban-rural geography are among the most policy-relevant aspects of the data. However, sub-group breakdowns carry re-identification risk (small cells at fine geographies) and can be politically misused if framed as rankings or name-and-shame comparisons. The project's binding guardrails require: sub-group breakdowns only where licensed and above the min-cell/denominator threshold; below-threshold cells suppressed and labeled (never imputed); no cross-dataset linkage; neutral framing with context and caveats, no ranking or name-and-shame. Builds on the four-indicator US dataset (011).
objective: Add suppression-safe sub-group disparities breakdowns (income, insurance status, race/ethnicity, urban-rural) to the dataset for available indicators and geographies, where licensed and above the min-cell/denominator threshold. Apply suppression rigorously; use neutral framing with context and caveats; no cross-dataset linkage.
acceptanceCriteria: Sub-group disparities breakdowns added for at least income and insurance status dimensions, and for race/ethnicity and urban-rural where available from licensed sources; each breakdown attributed to its specific source table and license

Small-cell guard strictly enforced: any sub-group cell below the configured minimum-cell or minimum-denominator threshold is suppressed and labeled (not imputed to a value); source suppression rules honored in addition to the project's own threshold
No cross-dataset linkage: sub-group breakdowns from different sources are not merged into a combined breakdown that could re-identify; each sub-group series cites its own source independently

Neutral framing enforced: dataset metadata and caveats use descriptive language (e.g., 'lower coverage rates observed among...') with no ranking, name-and-shame, or politically-loaded characterization; context and data limitations noted
All sub-group series are schema-valid per the canonical schema (task 004): harmonizationScore >= 90, basis labeled, provenanceComplete: true, suppression field correctly populated for all cells
CI reproduces all disparities breakdowns byte-stably from committed snapshots; 100% provenance coverage and license-status gate pass; small-cell guard CI gate enforces suppression threshold
Statistical reviewer and license/provenance reviewer sign-off recorded before publication
output: Published suppression-safe disparities breakdowns (income, insurance, race/ethnicity, urban-rural where available) added to the dataset: source-suppression and own-threshold rules enforced, no imputation, no cross-dataset linkage, neutral framing, schema-valid with harmonizationScore >= 90, reproducible from committed snapshots in CI; statistical and license reviewer sign-offs recorded.
